div.head: The Ambassador in Brazil (Caffery) to the Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
Rio de Janeiro, June 14, 1943—3 p.m.
[Received 3:53 p.m.]

div.p: 2874. For Rockefeller,Nelson A. Rockefeller, Coordinator of Inter-American Affairs. McClintock and RovenskyJohn C. McClintock and Joseph C. Rovensky, Assistant Coordinators of Inter-American Affairs. from Friele.Berent Friele, Representative of the Coordinator of Inter-American Affairs in Brazil. Foodstuffs shortage Bahia and other northeastern ports creating critical situation for whole propaganda position, since blame being placed Americans. Food Production Division contributing splendidly but time factor makes shipping problem extremely important for immediate future. Embassy detailed situation in telegram 2769 of June 7, 7 p.m. We strongly urge you lend every possible immediate support those recommendations. [Friele.]
